summary: Policy-backed control for whether remote clients can query this computer's Windows Search index. Good for reducing remote index exposure, but it also removes indexed-search convenience for network shares hosted on the machine.

setting.name: Remote Windows Search Queries
setting.casual_explanation: Windows Search can let other computers query this PC's search index over the network. This policy decides whether that remote index use is allowed.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it when they do not want other devices using this computer's search index, or when the machine should behave more like a standalone PC.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off reduces remote search exposure, but network clients lose indexed search behavior for shares hosted on this computer.
setting.professional_notes: Search.admx maps PreventRemoteQueries under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. Search.adml says enabled blocks remote index queries, disabled allows them, and the default behavior is disabled.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force the policy, and the default behavior allows client search requests to use this computer's index.

allowed_values.label: Remote queries allowed
allowed_values.meaning: Client search requests can use this computer's search index.

allowed_values.label: Remote queries blocked
allowed_values.meaning: Clients cannot query this computer's index remotely.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes PreventRemoteQueries = 1, which matches the documented block-remote-queries behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems exposing the Windows Search policy
windows_defaults.notes: Microsoft's local ADML help text says the default behavior is disabled, which means remote clients can use the index unless this policy is set to block them.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Systems that do not care about remote index exposure
avoid_for: Systems that should block remote index queries
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows Search to keep its normal policy baseline.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to prevent other computers from using this machine's index.
tradeoffs: Remote clients can still use indexed search behavior where supported.

recommended_profiles.label: No remote index queries
avoid_for: File servers or systems that rely on remote indexed-search behavior

states.rationale: Block remote clients from querying this computer's index.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want network clients kept out of this computer's search index.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want remote clients to benefit from indexed search behavior on this machine.
tradeoffs: Searching shared content from other devices can be less convenient.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft Search.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps PreventRemoteQueries under the Windows Search policy key with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft Search.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy blocks remote index queries, disabling it allows them, and the default behavior is disabled.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes PreventRemoteQueries = 1 for the current tweak.

decision.why: The local Microsoft Search policy files explicitly define the machine policy path HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search, the value name PreventRemoteQueries, and the enabled/disabled mapping. The app writes the documented enabled-state value 1 that blocks remote clients from querying the local search index.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="PreventRemoteQueries" class="Machine" ... key="SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search" valueName="PreventRemoteQueries"> <enabledValue><decimal value="1" /></enabledValue> <disabledValue><decimal value="0" /></disabledValue>; Search.adml: "If enabled, clients will be unable to query this computer's index remotely ... If disabled, client search requests will use this computer's index. Default is disabled."
validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft ADMX file defines the exact machine policy path and value name used by the app, with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. The paired ADML help text explains that enabling the policy blocks remote index queries and that the default behavior leaves them allowed.
